title: Ephemerantha trilamellata
intro: Ephemerantha trilamellata, also known as the three-layered orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ephemerantha trilamellata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ephemerantha trilamellata is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Ephemerantha trilamellata is classified in the family Orchidaceae and is native to tropical regions. It belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Coelogyneae. Further classification places it in the genus Ephemerantha. This orchid is often associated with the warmer climates of Asia and Africa. Within the Orchidaceae family, Ephemerantha trilamellata is a relatively understudied species.
